title: TypeScript Basics
description: Core types, interfaces, narrowing and safer JavaScript patterns.
explanation: TypeScript adds a static type layer to JavaScript so teams can model contracts, catch mistakes earlier and document intent directly in code.
interviewQuestions: How do type aliases differ from interfaces?
What is type narrowing and why is it useful?

codeExamples.title: Discriminated union

commonMistakes: Using any instead of modeling unknown data.
Duplicating backend contracts manually without shared validation.

title: React Components
description: Composable UI with props, state and focused rendering responsibilities.
explanation: React components should be small presentation units composed into features. Business logic belongs in hooks, stores, services or utilities.
interviewQuestions: What makes a React component reusable?
When should logic be extracted into a custom hook?

codeExamples.title: Typed component props

commonMistakes: Putting data-fetching details directly in every component.
Creating large components with unrelated responsibilities.

title: State Management
description: Client state, persisted preferences and feature-level derived data.
explanation: State management coordinates shared UI and progress data. Zustand keeps the MVP lightweight while supporting future service-backed synchronization.
interviewQuestions: What state belongs globally instead of locally?
How would you persist local-first progress safely?

codeExamples.title: Store action
codeExamples.code:
const markComplete = (id: string) =>
  set((state) => ({ completedTopicIds: [...state.completedTopicIds, id] }));

commonMistakes: Mixing server contracts with view-only state.

title: Client Routing
description: Route structure for dashboard, atlas, topics and practice modules.
explanation: React Router maps features to URLs and enables deep links for learning flows such as topic pages and quiz sessions.
interviewQuestions: Why are nested layouts useful in React Router?
How can lazy loading improve route performance?
commonMistakes: Hiding navigation state in components instead of URLs.
Creating routes without empty and loading states.
